question: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in each year from three years before the year (within 2017-2022) that has the highest average daily inmate population in the Allegheny County Jail census through that year, and also had 2020 Census populations under 100,000? List the city names in alphabetical order separated by `, `. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Filtering the Allegheny County Jail daily census data by year (from Date) for 2017-2022, the year with the highest average daily inmate population (_Count) is 2019 with an average of approximately 2572.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2017-2022 has the highest average daily inmate population in the Allegheny County Jail census? (Derive year from Date; compute average _Count by year and select the maximum.)

2.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer - 3> release, 10 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, and San Antonio.
2.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer - 3> release?

3.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer - 2> release, 12 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Baytown, Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Houston, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, and San Antonio.
3.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer - 2> release?

4.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer - 1> release, 12 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, El Paso, Houston, Laredo, Longview, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio, and Tyler.
4.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer - 1> release?

5.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer> release, 13 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Laredo, Longview, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio, Tyler, and Waco.
5.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer> release?

6.fact: According to the 2020 Census Redistricting Data for All Texas Places, the following 2020 Census populations (POP100 field) are recorded for the 9 cities from the intersection: Beaumont (115,282), Brownsville (186,738), Corpus Christi (317,863), El Paso (678,815), Laredo (255,205), McAllen (142,210), Mission (85,778), Pharr (79,715), and San Antonio (1,434,625). Only Mission and Pharr have populations under 100,000.
6.subquestion: Which cities from <intersection of nodes 2-5: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, El Paso, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio> had population under 100,000 in the 2020 Census?
